codeReview.summary: The feature delivers substantial export functionality and broad tests, but FCPXML path conversion has a correctness bug that breaks media references for relative source paths.

issues.description: _asset_src() drops the first path component by iterating path.parts[1:], so relative paths are truncated (e.g., source_file='videos/interview.mp4' becomes src='file:///interview.mp4'). This produces incorrect asset URIs and can prevent FCPXML imports from resolving media.

FCPXML src validation only asserts that src starts with 'file:///' and does not verify full path fidelity, so the truncation bug is not detected. Add path-correctness assertions for both relative and absolute source_file values.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires RED-first testing (or explicit deviation), but this run implemented export modules before creating tests while still reporting followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl skeleton for session 069b1850-fb3f-4756-8e27-9b5dc50d60d5 shows Create snipsnap/export/edl.py, fcpxml.py, davinci.py before Create tests/test_export.py; .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md lines 28-33 define RED-first and deviation reporting.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Relative source paths are a real pipeline case and should be treated as such in export logic/tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: snipsnap/transcription/base.py line 56 discovers paths via folder.rglob(...), and snipsnap/transcription/whisper_local.py line 91 persists source_file=str(video_path), which can be relative when folder_path is relative.

summary: Fail: FCPXML export has a blocking media-path bug for relative source paths; otherwise implementation and test breadth are strong.
